Title: A cytosolic bifunctional geranyl/farnesyl diphosphate synthase provides MVA-derived GPP for geraniol biosynthesis in rose flowers
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (constitutive, for transient overexpression in rose flowers and Nicotiana benthamiana leaves)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA911487; RNA-seq: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA911454; metabolite profiling (GC-MS, LC-MS/MS): no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: trans-short-chain isoprenyl diphosphate synthase; FPPS family; bifunctional geranyl/farnesyl diphosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic GPP and FPP supply for monoterpene and sesquiterpene volatile biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with DMAPP to produce GPP and further condenses IPP with GPP to produce FPP; bifunctional enzyme producing both GPP and FPP
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point and precursor-supplying step upstream of geraniol and germacrene D biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for cytosolic GPP supply; not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major contributor to pleasant rose floral scent; widely used in cosmetic and perfume industries
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rose flower petals/flowers (emitted volatile; intracellular GPP/FPP detected in petals)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes geraniol emission by supplying cytosolic GPP; also increases germacrene D emission via FPP supply and affects dhβ-ionol through cytosol-to-plastid metabolic crosstalk
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcG/FPPS1 is the only cytosolic rose IDS candidate that efficiently produces both GPP and FPP in vitro. 2. RNAi downregulation decreases geraniol, germacrene D, and dhβ-ionol emission, while overexpression increases them in rose flowers. 3. Coexpression with RcNUDX1-1a in Nicotiana benthamiana enhances geraniol glycoside accumulation, demonstrating in planta GPPS activity. 4. RcG/FPPS1 expression peaks at night and precedes rhythmic GPP/FPP accumulation and geraniol emission. 5. The enzyme explains the MVA-derived origin of cytosolic GPP for rose geraniol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in petals; rhythmic with maximum expression at night; relatively low abundance compared with RcNUDX1-1a
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Petals of flowers at anthesis/open flower stages; strong daily rhythmic expression peaking at night
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; day/night cycle; feeding with 13C-mevalonolactone and 2H2-deoxyxylulose; mevinolin and fosmidomycin inhibitor treatments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable isotope precursor feeding; pathway inhibitor treatments; subcellular fraction enzyme assays; transient subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme assays and kinetics; transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient RNAi knockdown and overexpression in rose flowers; metabolite profiling by GC-MS and LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression altered multiple terpenoid volatiles beyond geraniol, including germacrene D and dhβ-ionol, indicating broader isoprenoid flux perturbation and cytosol-plastid crosstalk
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance rose scent and geraniol production; likely useful for metabolic engineering of cytosolic monoterpene production, especially combined with RcNUDX1-1a; caution that manipulating RcG/FPPS1 also affects sesquiterpene and apocarotenoid-related volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homodimeric active enzyme; GPP/FPP output depends on IPP:DMAPP ratio. Two conserved residues, F88 and V123, are crucial for GPPS activity and evolved from ancestral FPPS-like states. Orthologous G/FPPS activity is conserved in Rosaceae.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (constitutive, for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA911487; RNA-seq: NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA911454
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: trans-short-chain isoprenyl diphosphate synthase; FPPS family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with allylic substrates to produce predominantly FPP; almost exclusively forms FPP from IPP and DMAPP/GPP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: FPP-derived sesquiterpenes/capsidiol and capsidiol acetate in heterologous assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides FPPS activity but does not effectively supply GPP for RcNUDX1-1a-dependent geraniol production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcFPPS2 is cytosolic and produces almost exclusively FPP in vitro. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana increases FPP-derived capsidiol/capsidiol acetate, confirming FPPS activity in planta. 3. Unlike RcG/FPPS1, it does not enhance geraniol glycosides with RcNUDX1-1a, indicating little functional GPPS contribution in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression in flowers/petals relative to RcNUDX1-1a
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro substrate assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a comparator or for engineering FPP-derived sesquiterpene flux rather than geraniol production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among experimentally characterized IDS candidates, but not the principal gene explaining geraniol biosynthesis.
